---
title: "Hoeller Lab"
subtitle: "Investigating what computations underlie vision"
---

How does the brain turn the ever-shifting two-dimensional images on the retina into a stable perception of a three-dimensional world? We tackle this question by combining mathematical theory, physics-inspired modeling, and close collaboration with experimentalists. We also harness artificial neural networks to uncover the computational principles unique to biological vision, and to design artificial architectures that capture them.

[More on research](research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .me-2} [Interested in joining?](join.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

## News

- **January 2027** — Lab starts at the University of Notre Dame!
